reviews.review_msg: She seems determined to make an easy course into something she thinks is more beneficial without putting in any effort. Only performs 1/3 of lectures while leaving the rest to the TA's and spends her lecture time talking about her accomplishments instead of course material. She was so bad that one of the TA's openly showed a disliking of her.

She cannot teach and force all staff to TAS. She force us to come to his lecture and she only shows off how good her company is. EXAM includes questions from guest lecture without any materials posted!!!!! They are very unfair and hard!
The only thing in ENSC 410 she did is to introduce how good her company is

Laziest professor I have encountered at SFU. The only reason she comes to class is to talk about herself, otherwise she relies on the TAs to on average teach twice per week. The TAs do all the heavy lifting. She also managed to turn an easy 3 credit hour course into a time consuming mess. How is she allowed to teach at SFU? I would give her a 0/5.

Once in a while, it is good to take up a course that allows you to breathe. Straightforward course. Attendance is mandatory. Follow instructions and actively participate. Prof is helpful if you keep her in the loop. I forgot to turn in an assignment and I emailed her and met her personally about it, she accepted it after the deadline.

Easy course. Good grader. Min effort required. Courses like this are necessary to relieve stress from tough courses or sems. Seminar based course, so you'll self learn If you skip classes, she will screw your grades. My project mate and I did good final report & presentation; got similar midterm scores yet he got A and I got B as I was irregular

I find her lectures pretty easy to follow and the assignments are also pretty straight forward. I agree with a previous feedback, not everything will be spoon-fed to you, and you will have to do your share of work. Overall, the course requires some work from you end on a regular basis and you should be good.

She seems like a very kind person at first. Despite her strong accent, you have hope for the semester, lured by her seemingly friendly smile. A week later you realize there is no warmth behind her smile. She grins like the Cheshire cat, a cold, mocking smirk. She straight up makes fun of students in class. Lectures are useless, just learn on ur own

shheeeeeee just doesn't get it.. I think she doesn't know why we (the students) are taking classes. She does tend to push her own philosophies and interests on the students (20% project about nanotechnology. This is not a nanotechnology class). You can't really rate her since this is an empty-feeling class. There's no material (no pun intended)

Terrible at teaching. Clearly doesn't understand the material herself. Wastes class time talking about herself and her accomplishments.  Can't answer questions in class. In previous semesters, she has intentionally withheld lecture notes from all students to punish those who no longer attended class. SFU shouldn't be letting this person near students
